channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: ICB clusters told to merge
item.description: Clustered integrated care boards should merge by next April, even if it is likely they will cut across future regional government footprints, NHS England has said.

item.title: ‘Attractiveness’ of ‘underpaid’ chair roles under review
item.description: The roles of trust and integrated care board chairs are being reviewed by NHS England to ensure they remain “coherent, effective and attractive to high-calibre leaders”, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Acute trust overturns ‘obviously unfair’ £1.7m legal bill
item.description: An acute trust has overturned a £1.7m legal bill in the High Court after a judge found “obvious unfairness” in an independent adjudicator’s decision about a legal dispute with a construction firm.

item.title: Theatres closed for months after ‘due diligence’ failures
item.description: Two new theatres were forced to shut for nearly six months after opening “without the necessary due diligence”, according to a review seen by HSJ .

item.title: Exclusive: Consultants revolt over group model
item.description: Consultants at a hospital trust have declared “no confidence” in senior leaders and called for a controversial group model to be split up, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Revealed: Cancer doctor hiring freezes rise in nearly every region
item.description: Cancer centres in nearly every region of England reported significant rises in recruitment freezes to oncology posts over the past year, according to new figures shared with HSJ .

item.title: Public inquiry into maternity being considered by DHSC
item.description: Current inquiries into care failures lack teeth, and such a gap could be filled by a public inquiry, the government’s new national maternity adviser has revealed.

item.title: We have ‘minimised’ Health Bill power grab, says minister
item.description: The government has sought to “minimise” the new powers given to the health and social care secretary in its Health Bill, according to the minister leading it through Parliament.

item.title: Trusts ‘should recruit more managers locally’
item.description: NHS organisations should recruit more managers and leaders from local communities rather than conventional entry routes, health minister Karin Smyth told HSJ .

item.title: Charity commits £250m to ‘neighbourhood health’
item.description: A charity will invest £250m over the next three to five years in a government-backed scheme testing a new funding model for neighbourhood health.

item.title: Minister apologises for NHSE Palantir data blunder
item.description: A health minister has apologised for NHS England wrongly telling a watchdog that Palantir had no access to patient information in the federated data platform

item.title: Boss leaves troubled trust after six months
item.description: The managing director of one of England’s worst-performing trusts is leaving after just six months, in a move her boss acknowledged could raise “understandable questions”.

item.title: Tech firms asked to shoulder more risk in NHS contracts
item.description: The Department of Health and Social Care wants tech suppliers to take on more financial risk by agreeing to new contract models aimed at improving value, HSJ understands .

item.title: ICB ‘owes council north of £40m’ in care dispute
item.description: An integrated care board owes a council “north of £40m” for the costs of accommodating patients after discharge, a councillor has claimed.

item.title: Trust fined £320k over hospital infection death
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has imposed a £324,143 fine on a trust where a chemotherapy patient contracted a serious infection from bacteria in a ward’s en-suite bathroom and later died.

item.title: NHS CEO might not report to health secretary
item.description: The new NHS chief executive may soon report to a senior civil servant rather than the health secretary, HSJ understands.

item.title: CEO who said he wouldn’t ‘go on forever’ returns as group boss
item.description: A trust CEO who said he had chosen not to “keep going forever” when he retired at the age of 70 in 2024 is to return as the interim leader of a struggling hospital group.

item.title: Bereaved families raise alarm about trust’s leadership churn
item.description: Bereaved parents have raised concerns about the departure of a major trust’s medical director, just as an independent inquiry into its maternity services is getting started.

item.title: Trust forced to wait 18 months for CQC report
item.description: A Care Quality Commission report into an ambulance trust is still yet to be published more than 18 months after the inspectors visited, in what is thought to be the longest delay of its kind.

item.title: Resident doctors halt strikes after government pay offer
item.description: Resident doctors in England have called off strikes due to take place this month after the government made them a new contract offer.
